# TuneBad

> TuneBad is a free, all-in-one music toolkit that runs in the browser. It finds the key, BPM, and loudness of any song, converts YouTube, Spotify, and SoundCloud links to MP3, WAV, or MP4, makes slowed + reverb edits, shifts pitch, and calculates delay times. There are no ads, no accounts, and no paywalls. Audio analysis happens locally in the user's browser, so files are never uploaded.

Key facts:
- Analysis (key, BPM, loudness) runs client-side on the essentia audio engine, the same engine used by leading key/BPM tools.
- BPM results show both the detected tempo and its half/double (for example "150 or 75") because many songs can be read at either.
- Results include musical key, Camelot code for harmonic mixing, energy, danceability, and loudness in dBFS.
- Harmonic mixing is built in: every song page in the database lists which Camelot codes mix cleanly with the track and links compatible-key and same-key songs to play next, and the playlist analyzer can sort a whole set into Camelot-wheel order.
- Values are measured fresh from the audio with essentia at analysis time, not served from a pre-computed catalog.
- The converter supports YouTube, Spotify, SoundCloud, TikTok, Instagram, X (Twitter), Bandcamp, Vimeo, Mixcloud, and Audiomack, for personal use only.
- Available in 8 languages: English, French, Spanish, German, Portuguese, Italian, Japanese, and Chinese.

## Tools

- [Key & BPM Finder](https://www.tunebad.com/key-bpm-finder): Search a song by name, upload an audio file, or paste a YouTube, Spotify, or SoundCloud link, and get the key, tempo, Camelot code, energy, and loudness in a few seconds, plus a list of songs that mix well with it.
- [Camelot Wheel](https://www.tunebad.com/camelot-wheel): Interactive chart of all 24 Camelot codes (1A-12A minor, 1B-12B major). Click a code to see its musical key, which codes mix cleanly with it, and real songs in that key.
- [Playlist Analyzer](https://www.tunebad.com/playlist-analyzer): Paste a Spotify or YouTube playlist link and get the key, BPM, and Camelot code for every track in one table, ready to export as CSV or sort into Camelot-wheel order for DJ sets.
- [MP3 Cutter](https://www.tunebad.com/mp3-cutter): Trim a song to the part you want, with click-to-play scrubbing and a live, audible fade in and out, then save it as an MP3 or WAV ringtone.
- [Converter](https://www.tunebad.com/converter): Turn a YouTube, Spotify, or SoundCloud link into an MP3, WAV, or MP4 file. Also converts local audio files in the browser.
- [Loudness Penalty & LUFS Meter](https://www.tunebad.com/loudness): Measure a track's loudness with the BS.1770 standard, see how much Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube, TIDAL, Amazon, and Deezer will turn it down, then normalize and export to a target loudness.
- [Slowed + Reverb Maker](https://www.tunebad.com/slowed-reverb): Slow a track down (or speed it up for nightcore) and add one of five reverb types with a draggable parametric EQ, then export it.
- [Frequency to Note Calculator](https://www.tunebad.com/pitch-shifter): Convert any frequency in Hz to the nearest musical note, octave, and cents offset. To actually change a song's pitch, use the Nightcore Maker or Slowed + Reverb Maker.
- [Delay & Reverb Calculator](https://www.tunebad.com/delay-reverb-calculator): Enter a BPM and get delay times in milliseconds and Hz, from 1/1 down to 1/64, including dotted and triplet values.
- [BPM Tap Tempo & Metronome](https://www.tunebad.com/bpm-tap): Tap along to find a song's BPM, or use the built-in metronome.
- [More Tools hub](https://www.tunebad.com/tools): Free file utilities that run entirely in the browser, nothing is uploaded.
- [Image Converter](https://www.tunebad.com/image-converter): Convert PNG, JPG, and WebP images in the browser.
- [Compress Image](https://www.tunebad.com/compress-image): Shrink JPG/PNG/WebP file sizes with a quality slider.
- [Resize Image](https://www.tunebad.com/resize-image): Resize images to exact pixel dimensions, with aspect-ratio lock.
- [Resize for Instagram](https://www.tunebad.com/resize-image-for-instagram): Exact Instagram sizes, Story 1080x1920, post 1080x1080, portrait 1080x1350, landscape 1080x566.
- [Compress Image to 100KB](https://www.tunebad.com/compress-image-to-100kb): Compress a photo under an exact size cap (50KB, 100KB, 200KB, or 500KB).
- [HEIC to JPG](https://www.tunebad.com/heic-to-jpg): Convert HEIC/HEIF photos from an iPhone to JPG or PNG in the browser, no upload.
- [Merge PDF](https://www.tunebad.com/merge-pdf): Combine several PDF files into one document, in any order, without uploading them.
- [Split PDF](https://www.tunebad.com/split-pdf): Extract a page range from a PDF into a new file, in the browser, no upload.
- [JPG to PDF](https://www.tunebad.com/jpg-to-pdf): Turn JPG and PNG images into a single PDF, one page per photo.
- [Unzip Files](https://www.tunebad.com/unzip-files): Open a ZIP, TAR, or TAR.GZ archive and download its contents, or bundle files into a new ZIP or TAR.GZ.
- [Compress Video](https://www.tunebad.com/compress-video): Shrink a video to 10, 25, 50, or 100 MB in the browser, no upload.
- [Compress Video for Discord](https://www.tunebad.com/compress-video-for-discord): Get a clip under Discord's 10MB limit in the browser.
- [Video Converter](https://www.tunebad.com/video-converter): Convert a video to MP4, WebM, MKV, MOV, AVI, FLV, or WMV in the browser, no upload.
- [Compress Video for WhatsApp](https://www.tunebad.com/compress-video-for-whatsapp): Get a video under WhatsApp's 16MB share limit in the browser.
- [Audio Converter](https://www.tunebad.com/audio-converter): Convert audio to MP3 (128/192/320 kbps), WAV, FLAC, OGG, or M4A in the browser, no upload.
- [MKV to MP4](https://www.tunebad.com/mkv-to-mp4): Convert MKV files (rips, OBS recordings) to MP4 in the browser, no upload.
- [MOV to MP4](https://www.tunebad.com/mov-to-mp4): Convert iPhone/QuickTime MOV videos to MP4 in the browser, no upload.
- [FLAC to MP3](https://www.tunebad.com/flac-to-mp3): Convert lossless FLAC to MP3 at 128/192/320 kbps in the browser, no upload.
- [WAV to MP3](https://www.tunebad.com/wav-to-mp3): Convert uncompressed WAV to MP3 at 128/192/320 kbps in the browser, no upload.
- [Audio Mastering](https://www.tunebad.com/audio-mastering): Master a song in the browser with EQ, glue compression, and a loudness limiter to a streaming (-14 LUFS) or loud (-9 LUFS) target, or match a reference track's tone and loudness. Nothing is uploaded.
- [Nightcore Maker](https://www.tunebad.com/nightcore-maker): Speed a track up and pitch it up together for a nightcore edit, in the browser, no upload.
- [Bass Booster](https://www.tunebad.com/bass-booster): Boost the low end of a song with a low-shelf bass boost and a safety limiter so it never clips.
- [8D Audio Maker](https://www.tunebad.com/8d-audio): Add a slow auto-panning "8D" effect for headphones, in the browser, no upload.
- [Audio Joiner](https://www.tunebad.com/audio-joiner): Merge two or more audio files into one, reorder them, and export as MP3 or WAV.

## Guides

- [How to Find the Key and BPM of Any Song](https://www.tunebad.com/guides/find-key-and-bpm-of-any-song): Step-by-step guide to detecting a track's key and tempo, by ear or with the free analyzer.
- [The Camelot Wheel, Explained](https://www.tunebad.com/guides/camelot-wheel-harmonic-mixing): How the Camelot code system works and how DJs use it for harmonic mixing.
- [How to Make a Slowed + Reverb Edit](https://www.tunebad.com/guides/how-to-make-slowed-and-reverb): Making a slowed + reverb (or nightcore) edit for free in the browser.
- [What Is LUFS? Streaming Loudness, Explained](https://www.tunebad.com/guides/what-is-lufs-streaming-loudness): What LUFS means, how streaming loudness normalization works, and what to master to.
- [How to Make a Ringtone From Any Song](https://www.tunebad.com/guides/how-to-make-a-ringtone): Picking the right 20-30 second section, trimming with fades, and exporting an MP3/WAV ringtone in the browser, plus the honest steps for getting it onto an iPhone as m4r.

## Pages

- [Home](https://www.tunebad.com/): Overview of every tool, a one-line tour of each one, and a FAQ.
- [Song Key & BPM Database](https://www.tunebad.com/songs): Browse the key, BPM, and Camelot code for songs analyzed on TuneBad. Each song has its own page at /song/<slug> with a harmonic-mixing section (which Camelot codes mix with it, plus linked compatible-key and same-key songs). Browse hubs: every musical key at /songs/key/<key> (for example /songs/key/g-sharp-minor), every Camelot code at /songs/camelot/<code> (for example /songs/camelot/8a), common tempos at /songs/bpm/<n> (for example /songs/bpm/140), curated activity tempo ranges at /songs/bpm-for/<activity> (running, workout, study-focus, and more), and artists with two or more analyzed songs at /artist/<slug>. Values are measured from official 30-second previews with the essentia engine.
- [TuneBad vs Tunebat](https://www.tunebad.com/tunebad-vs-tunebat): Honest comparison. TuneBad analyzes the actual audio (any file or link, free, no ads, no signup); Tunebat looks songs up in a large database. Use TuneBad for remixes, edits, and unreleased tracks a database will not have.
- [Copyright Disclaimer](https://www.tunebad.com/copyright): Terms of use and the policy on copyrighted material.
